items.title: Switzerland’s Inflation Slows for First Time in Eight Months
items.description: Swiss inflation slowed in June for the first time in eight months, suggesting the impact of lower oil costs are feeding through to the domestic economy.

items.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi met Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi in New Delhi during her first official visit to the country, as the two leaders aim to deepen strategic and economic ties

items.title: China’s Private Refiners Snap Up Middle East Oil as Prices Slide
items.description: China’s independent refiners are taking advantage of cheaper Middle Eastern oil after flows accelerated through the Strait of Hormuz, snapping up barrels from producers including Saudi Arabia and Iraq

items.title: Carlsberg Said to File for India IPO to Raise $700 Million
items.description: Carlsberg A/S has confidentially filed draft papers for an initial public offering of its India unit to raise as much as $700 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: FTSE 100 Set For Second Day of Declines
Yen Edges Higher With Traders on Alert for Risk of Intervention
items.description: The yen strengthened slightly against the dollar Thursday amid speculation that further intervention by Japan to bolster the currency may come without warning from officials.

items.title: Palm Oil Dips on Weaker Crude and Malaysian Production Outlook
items.description: Palm oil edged lower, weighed down by declining crude prices and expectations for strong output from the second-biggest grower, Malaysia.

items.title: Foreign Traders Sell Biggest Pile of Japan Bonds in Three Years
items.description: Overseas investors offloaded the most Japanese bonds in three years in June, official data showed, after a lackluster performance left the debt trailing most of its global peers.

items.description: The bond market is taking him seriously, but that’s not necessarily great news.
